title: rubus arrhenii subsp. arrhenianthus
intro: Rubus arrhenii subsp. arrhenianthus, also known as the arrhenian raspberry or dwarf raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus arrhenii plant, which belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus arrhenii subsp. arrhenianthus include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Rubus arrhenii subsp. arrhenianthus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
